citations.description: Step 3 still shows entirely manual recipe with no automation language

findings.criterion: Update docs/releasing.md step 3 to mark Homebrew automated (manual = fallback)
findings.detail: docs/releasing.md step 3 (lines 310–326) still contains the entirely manual recipe: it instructs the operator to curl PyPI for the sdist url+sha256, edit Formula/rebar.rb manually, and git-commit+push in the tap repo. No language of 'automated (scheduled self-bump)', 'autobump', or any reference to the self-bump workflow appears anywhere in the file. The attestation comment claims this landed in 'Gerrit 859 / origin/main 0ea16f5f4', but the codebase at HEAD does not reflect that change.

citations.description: version '0.9.0' in both top-level and packages[]

findings.criterion: Release v0.9.1: pyproject.toml and server.json bumped to 0.9.1, CHANGELOG [0.9.1] entry present
findings.detail: pyproject.toml version = '0.9.0' (line 10), server.json version = '0.9.0' (line 5 + line 15), and CHANGELOG.md starts at [0.9.0] with no [0.9.1] section. The v0.9.1 release version bump has not landed in the repository. The attestation comment claims pyproject+server.json were bumped to 0.9.1 and published, but the codebase does not reflect this.

data.remediation: How to resolve the unmet criteria: for each one, decide whether the work is genuinely incomplete or simply undocumented. If a requirement is already satisfied but the proof is not discoverable from the code alone, add a comment to this ticket that documents the evidence — cite the concrete artifacts that meet the requirement (file paths and line ranges, commands and their output, links, or the reasoning that ties the work to the criterion). The completion verifier reads this ticket's comments, so evidence you record there is taken into account on the next verification. For any criterion whose work is genuinely unfinished, complete it, then re-verify.
